name: Hsinchu County (Taiwan)

colors.symbolism: the sky over the Hsinchu plain and the Taiwan Strait coast
the bamboo for which Hsinchu (新竹, 'New Bamboo') is named, and the surrounding forests

description: A light-blue field charged at the centre with a circular emblem composed of a green-and-orange ring within which the stylised characters '新竹' (Hsinchu) appear in white.
history: Hsinchu County (Xinzhu Xian) lies on Taiwan's northwest coast and surrounds Hsinchu City (separately administered as a provincial city). The current county flag bearing the multi-coloured emblem with the characters 新竹 ('New Bamboo') has been in use since the late 20th century. The county is the historic homeland of much of Taiwan's Hakka population.

features.symbolism: circular green-and-orange emblem with the characters 新竹 (Hsinchu, 'New Bamboo') at the centre, evoking the bamboo groves of the Hakka highlands and the rising sun over the county
